kamloops.title: Kelowna man fined $5K for flying drone during 2023 McDougall Creek wildfire
kamloops.description: A Kelowna man has been ordered to pay a $5,000 fine for flying a drone near the fire-ravaged Okanagan Lake Resort during the McDougall Creek wildfire in 2023.

all.title: Wildfire magazine – latest issue
all.description: Wildfire magazine, Issue 4, 2025. Wildfire is published quarterly for the greater global wildland fire community and for members of the International Association of Wildland Fire. The magazine serves as a storytelling and network building platform, exemplifying our role as a unique supporter and unifier of fire professionals across the globe. https://issuu.com/wildfiremagazine-iawf/docs/wildfire-m

all.title: When are wildfire evacuations most dangerous? Research finds leaving late risks lives
all.description: “Once you’re on the road, it may already be too late.” The somber warning was recently given by researchers who analyzed hundreds of self-recorded videos from people evacuating from wildfires. They were studying driving behaviour during emergency evacuations to learn why and when people choose to evacuate and understand how those decisions put lives at […]

all.title: This is why we train – chainsaw graduates get inspired
all.description: Trainers like what they do, and they are going to tell you why. At Yukon First Nations Wildfire, training is all about helping people and being safe. Here’s a bit of inspiration and enthusiasm from graduates of the recent Chainsaw Train the Trainer course, out of Whitehorse, Yukon Territory, Canada.

all.title: Fire burns down historic building at key fire research complex
all.description: A historic home at a world-leading fire research complex was consumed by a lightning fire on Tuesday and burnt to the ground. The historic Beadel House, on the Tall Timbers Research Station and Land Conservancy complex near Tallahassee, Florida in the United States, was completely lost despite the efforts of the Tallahassee Fire Department. One […]

all.title: WFCC returns to Canada in 2026
all.description: The combined Wildland Fire Canada Conference and Canadian Smoke Forum unites wildland fire management agencies, Indigenous knowledge holders, scientists and community members from across Canada and around the world. This biennial conference focuses on wildland fire management, science and knowledge sharing with the latest research, technology and practices. The last WFCC was in 2024 in […]

all.title: Production to restart for the 214ST helicopter
all.description: A new and improved Bell 214ST helicopter will soon roll off the production lines with McDermott Aviation announcing the restart of manufacturing after more than 20 years for one of the main workhorses of disaster response worldwide. McDermott Aviation is announcing the move at the Verticon aviation conference in Atlanta, US, this week. Australian-based McDermott […]

all.title: Perspectives from the IAWF president
all.description: A conversation with Trevor Howard, National Manager Prescribed Burning Strategy, AFAC and President of the International Association of Wildland Fire, with AFAC Deputy CEO and Executive Director, Strategy, People and Performance Erin Liston-Abel.
